This man drove a $1,000,000 Bugatti into a lagoon for a $2.2M insurance payout and blamed it on a pelican Andy House owned an exotic car salvage yard in Texas He bought a 2006 Bugatti Veyron for $1 million in October 2009 Three weeks later he insured it for $2.2 million as a “rare collector’s item” One month after that he drove it straight into Gulf Bay near La Marque, Texas When cops arrived he said a low flying pelican startled him and he swerved into the water What he didn’t know was that a random driver had been filming the Bugatti from his car the whole time because he’d never seen one before “That’ll be mine one day” the man said on camera Right before the Bugatti disappeared into the lagoon No pelican in the footage He had also left the engine running after impact to suck in saltwater and guarantee a total loss He filed the $2.2 million claim the next morning The video went viral The FBI, Texas Rangers and local police all launched investigations simultaneously He faced 20 years He got one year, three years probation and had to pay back every dollar A stranger who just wanted to film a cool car ended a $2.2 million fraud The pelican was never found

exploring more projects building on @arc and today i checked out @TowerExchange btw, Tower is invite-only for now and i have got a few access codes to share. indicate if you need one thing i noticed immediately is that they’re not trying to be “just another dex” Tower is built as a dex aggregator. instead of pulling liquidity from only one source, it searches across routes to help users get better trades. sounds simple, but this actually matters a lot. because one thing we don’t realize is: two people swapping the same token at the same time can end up with completely different prices depending on where liquidity is sourced from. so having a system that automatically looks for better routes makes trading smoother. another thing i found interesting is the recurring buy/sell ai agent feature. basically users can set strategies ahead of time and let the system execute recurring buys or sells automatically. kind of like setting your own onchain dca strategy instead of manually opening charts every day. they also added bridging directly into the platform, which makes sense honestly. what i like here is that the product feels focused on convenience: - better trade routing - automation tools - bridging all inside one experience link : https://t.co/lHEiOkpy62 still early, but definitely one of the more practical trading-focused products i’ve explored on arc so far. will be exploring TrustCircle next, Be There 🫵
